description: Labeled environmental claims for greenwashing classifier. label=1 means vague/unsubstantiated (greenwashing), label=0 means specific/verifiable (credible). 140 samples total.

samples.text: We are committed to sustainability and a greener future.
Our project has world-class environmental standards.
We are an industry leader in climate action.
This project contributes to a more sustainable world.
100% of our carbon credits are high quality and additional.
Our methodology is best in class.
We are making significant progress toward net zero.
Our company takes environmental responsibility very seriously.
The project delivers positive environmental impact.
We have deeply integrated sustainability into everything we do.
Our solutions are environmentally friendly and eco-conscious.
This initiative represents a transformative step in climate leadership.
We work tirelessly to protect forests around the world.
Our approach is holistic and deeply committed to nature.
We have robust safeguards and world-leading oversight.
Our carbon offsets deliver meaningful climate benefits.
The project has generated substantial environmental co-benefits.
We are pioneers in nature-based climate solutions.
Our operations are designed with the planet in mind.
We empower local communities to steward the land sustainably.
This REDD+ project has meaningfully reduced pressure on the forest.
Our credits represent genuine, lasting climate impact.
We are deeply engaged in the fight against climate change.
The project aligns with the highest international standards.
We continuously innovate to maximize climate benefit.
Our forest protection strategy is best-in-class and science-driven.
Stakeholder engagement is at the heart of our approach.
We ensure environmental integrity through rigorous processes.
This project truly exemplifies sustainable land use.
Our carbon accounting is transparent and comprehensive.
We deliver climate action at unprecedented scale.
Our project area has remained largely intact thanks to our efforts.
We are proud to contribute to global climate goals.
Sustainability is in our DNA.
Our forest management is aligned with global best practices.
This carbon project is a game-changer for environmental protection.
We are committed to making a real difference for the planet.
Our project delivers transformative social and environmental outcomes.
We have implemented industry-leading monitoring and verification.
The project sets a new benchmark for carbon market integrity.
Our community engagement model is the gold standard.
This initiative demonstrates our deep commitment to climate action.
We are at the forefront of innovative conservation finance.
The project has created a thriving ecosystem for biodiversity.
Our approach to forest conservation is unmatched in the industry.
We ensure that every tonne of CO2 reduction is real and permanent.
Our project is a model for sustainable development at scale.
We leverage cutting-edge technology to maximize environmental impact.
The forest is thriving under our stewardship.
Our credits are backed by the highest quality environmental outcomes.
We remain deeply committed to protecting biodiversity hotspots.
Our REDD+ activities are delivering exceptional results.
Communities are at the center of our conservation mission.
We took bold action to prevent deforestation across the region.
Our project demonstrates how nature and communities can thrive together.
We are delivering world-class environmental and social outcomes.
Our forward-thinking approach ensures lasting climate benefits.
The project has achieved its environmental objectives and more.
We partner with local leaders to deliver impactful conservation results.
Our verification process is the most rigorous available.
We have taken robust action to ensure additionality.
The project continues to deliver exceptional climate benefits.
Our forest cover is substantially protected due to our activities.
We remain committed to responsible and transparent carbon accounting.
The project is making a real contribution to halting climate change.
Our project has been independently verified to the highest standards.
We ensure all credits are of the highest environmental integrity.
The project is aligned with global climate commitments.
Our conservation work is delivering benefits across the whole region.
We are proud to stand behind the quality and impact of every credit.
The project retained 87.3% of baseline forest cover between 2016 and 2023.
We issued 145,320 VCUs based on measured biomass stocks in 2022.
Deforestation rate in the project zone decreased from 2.1% to 0.4% annually.
A field audit by SCS Global Services covered 324 hectares across 18 plots.
Baseline emissions were calculated using VM0007 methodology version 1.6.
Above-ground biomass was measured at 142 tC per hectare via LiDAR in 2021.
The project boundary covers 214,503 hectares of tropical moist forest in Madre de Dios.
Leakage was estimated at 12.4% using the VT0001 tool and discounted from net credits.
Annual monitoring reports filed with Verra on 14 March 2022 and 9 April 2023.
Remote sensing analysis used Landsat 8 Tier 1 surface reflectance at 30m resolution.
Project start date: 1 January 2012; crediting period: 30 years; first verification: 2015.
Community benefits agreement signed with 47 indigenous communities totaling 18,200 residents.
Between 2015 and 2020, canopy cover declined from 92% to 76% per Hansen et al.
Total credits issued to date: 2,847,312 VCUs; retired: 1,932,004.
The buffer pool contribution is set at 20% per the AFOLU non-permanence risk tool.
Monitoring plots: 124 permanent sample plots of 0.1 hectare each.
Baseline deforestation reference region totals 1.4 million hectares.
Carbon stock uncertainty was estimated at ±12.3% at the 90% confidence level.
The project employs 34 full-time forest patrol staff across 6 patrol routes.
Validation was conducted by AENOR Internacional S.A.U. in November 2019.
Net GHG emission reductions in 2022: 412,847 tCO2e, after buffer deduction.
Satellite-detected forest loss in the project area was 1,243 hectares in 2021.
The project uses Sentinel-2 imagery with monthly compositing and cloud masking.
Biomass equations used: Chave et al. 2014 for tropical moist forest biomass.
IRR for project developer: 14.2% after accounting for 20% buffer contribution.
The project implemented fire-break maintenance over 312 km of perimeter in 2022.
Ex-ante estimate: 24.3 million tCO2e over 30-year crediting period.
Ground-truth biomass samples: n=186, collected between May and August 2020.
Revenue sharing: 60% to community trust, 25% to project developer, 15% to government.
The reference region experienced 2.8% annual deforestation from 2006-2011.
Methodology VM0015 applied with deviation approved by Verra on 2018-07-21.
Forest cover in the leakage belt increased by 3.1% from 2018 to 2023.
Buffer pool held: 284,310 tCO2e as of last issuance.
Average tree density: 412 stems per hectare with 28% basal area in Shorea spp.
Patrol records show 147 illegal logging incidents intercepted in 2022.
Three independent verifications completed between 2016 and 2023 by Rainforest Alliance.
Total project area: 386,240 hectares; forested area at baseline: 321,918 hectares (83.3%).
Annual canopy loss measured at 0.31% per year from 2018 to 2022 via Hansen GFC v1.10.
NDVI trend analysis shows mean decline of 0.042 units from 2016 to 2023 in degraded zones.
Carbon density map produced using ALOS-2 PALSAR-2 at 25m resolution in April 2022.
Project issued 1,240,000 ARB offset credits between 2014 and 2022 under CARB protocol.
Soil carbon stocks sampled at 48 plots to 1m depth; mean SOC: 121 tC/ha.
Logging moratorium covers all coupes within 5 km of permanent stream networks.
The project area experienced zero satellite-confirmed fire events in 2021 (NASA VIIRS).
Biodiversity assessment conducted over 14 days in June 2022 recorded 47 mammal species.
Leakage displacement risk assessed at 8% using USDA Forest Service spatial model.
Ground inventory: 218 circular 0.04-ha plots established in stratified random design.
Community distribution: $4.30 per tCO2e to village conservation funds upon each issuance.
Verification body: Bureau Veritas Certification, audit dates 12-19 September 2022.
Mean annual increment for dominant Pinus ponderosa stands: 3.2 m3/ha/yr (2019-2022).
Official land title: Government of Tanzania Certificate of Customary Right of Occupancy No. 7821.
Peat subsidence rate measured at monitoring wells: mean 1.8 cm/yr from 2017 to 2022.
Monitoring report MR-2022 submitted to Gold Standard registry on 28 February 2023.
Allometric model: Feldpausch et al. 2012 pantropical equations applied to all strata.
Forest patrol GPS tracks totaling 8,342 km logged in the SMART system for 2022.
Carbon price hedged at $12.50/tCO2e under a 7-year forward sale agreement signed 2021.
Orangutan nest density: 4.2 nests per km2 in 2022 survey, up from 3.1 nests per km2 in 2017.
Additionality confirmed: counterfactual land use is oil palm conversion, NPV $3,840/ha.
Fire management budget: $186,000 allocated in FY2023; 14 water tankers pre-positioned.
Stratified random sampling design with 95% confidence interval gives ±4.8% uncertainty.
